page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-128 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon CPU with an x86_64 architecture. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 64 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes an L3 cache of 54 MB, alongside L2 and L1d caches of 1 MB and 48 KB respectively. The system is equipped with 864.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.75 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it does not feature hardware GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. In multi-core processing, the instance achieved a strong rating with a stress-ng multi-core score of 120,775.82 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17,383.0. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 6,610.59 GB/sec and large block uncached reads at 329.21 GB/sec. Cryptographic operations also performed in the top tier, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 104,927.78. However, single-core performance remains average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,579.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1,845.38.

From a resource density perspective, the n2-highmem-128 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large memory footprints without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage, and weak performance in large-scale LLM text generation, where it managed only 2.68 tokens/sec on a 70B model. Conversely, it demonstrated strong capabilities in database operations, software compilation, and medium-model prompt processing. This profile makes the instance suitable for large-scale database hosting, memory-intensive enterprise applications, software compilation pipelines, and static web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-128 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon cores and 864.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.6 GHz, the processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core and memory performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 120,775.82 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 329.21 GB/sec. Cryptographic execution is top-tier, though single-core performance is average. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads, though the lack of local storage requires network-attached storage. It is suitable for large-scale database operations, software compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-128 is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 864 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-128 is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 864 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring high-density RAM and strong multi-core processing for enterprise databases and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs and 864 GB of system memory
Strong multi-core performance and high memory read bandwidth
Top-tier cryptographic execution and database operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for large databases, software compilation, and web serving
